items.title: Bitcoin Flies Above $104,000 After Powell Remarks, Stocks Remain Stunned By AI Concerns
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Bitcoin jumped 3% to $104,000 after Fed Chair Powell took a surprisingly open stance on crypto assets, while Nvidia stock dropped 6% due to reports of stricter chip export curbs to China.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia stock dropped nearly 6% following reports that President Trump is pushing for stricter restrictions on U.S. chip exports to China.
The S&P 500 index, tracked by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, closed 0.45% down for the day, broadly unchanged from levels before the Fed meeting.
The Nasdaq 100, followed by the Invesco QQQ Trust, finished 0.2% lower.
The Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund closed 0.4% higher, outperforming the other sectors but remaining broadly flat following Powell's remarks.

items.title: First Interest Rate Cut May Not Come Until June 'If At All,' Economist Warns As Fed Holds Steady
items.author: Erica Kollmann
items.description: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, with economists noting a slightly more hawkish tone in the policy statement. Experts suggest the first potential rate cut may not come until June or later, if at all, as the Fed waits to see the impact of policy changes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF, which tracks the S&P 500 index, was down 0.45% on the day, indicating a negative market reaction to the Fed's decision to hold rates steady.
The Invesco QQQ Trust, which tracks the Nasdaq 100 index, was down 0.19% on the day, also suggesting a negative market response to the Fed's policy announcement.

items.title: Fed Holds Interest Rates Steady, Halts Streak Of Consecutive Cuts, Says Inflation Remains 'Elevated' (UPDATED)
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged at 4.25%-4.5%, ending a streak of three consecutive rate cuts. The central bank acknowledged that inflation remains elevated, but said the risks to its employment and inflation goals are balanced.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The S&P 500 ETF fluctuated near the flatline, holding session losses at 0.6%.
The real estate sector was the day's worst performer, extending losses to 1.3%.
Nvidia shares extended their losses, plunging 6% as the Trump administration vowed to impose further chip export restrictions on China.

items.title: US Futures Point To Cautious Start As Investors Await Fed's Rate Decision: Tesla, Microsoft, Meta Among Stocks In Focus Ahead Of Earnings
items.author: Rishabh Mishra
items.description: U.S. stock futures were mixed ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and key earnings reports from major tech companies. Investors are cautious as they await the Fed's policy announcement and guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Tesla's stock was down 0.49% in the premarket ahead of its earnings release, indicating a neutral sentiment as investors await the company's financial results.
Microsoft's stock was down 0.16% in the premarket, suggesting a neutral sentiment as the market awaits the company's earnings report.
Meta Platforms' stock was up 0.68% in the premarket, indicating a positive sentiment as investors anticipate the company's upcoming earnings release.

Starbucks' stock was down 0.39% despite the company posting better-than-expected first-quarter financial results, suggesting a negative sentiment due to the company's suspension of its fiscal year 2025 guidance.
Stryker's stock was down 1.30% after the company reported better-than-expected earnings but announced the retirement of its CFO, indicating a negative sentiment.
60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals' stock was up 81.63% after the company received IRB approval for a Phase II study, suggesting a positive sentiment.

Silexion Therapeutics' stock was up 37.04% after the company reported new preclinical data for its drug candidate, indicating a positive sentiment.

Banzai International's stock was up 12.80% following the company's announcement of an acquisition, suggesting a positive sentiment.

The SPDR S&P 500 ETF was up 0.11% in premarket trading, indicating a positive sentiment.
The Invesco QQQ Trust ETF was up 0.45% in premarket trading, suggesting a positive sentiment.
